summary: Iteration 219: Notification plumbing fully tested. WhatsApp (8 templates) + Email (5 templates) services import cleanly, idempotency wired, HTML renders correctly. All trigger points confirmed: signup→welcome (auth_routes.py line 326), Razorpay→order_confirmed (server.py ~20030), concierge ticket→concierge_request (mira_service_desk.py ~759). Proactive alerts GET /api/mira/proactive/alerts/{pet_id} returns 200 with correct structure. MiraOSPage.jsx pet flickering fix (loadUserPets useEffect) and TODAY tab URL fix (/api/mira/proactive/alerts/) both confirmed in source. WHATSAPP_TEMPLATES_APPROVED=false active (freeform mode). Bug found and fixed: attach_or_create_ticket returned 500 when initial_message=None. 37/37 tests pass.

minor.issue: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'text' at mira_service_desk.py line 717 — when initial_message=None (force_new=True without initial_message payload). Fixed: null-safe access for request.initial_message.text

action_items: Bug was found and fixed in this session: mira_service_desk.py line 717 now null-safe for initial_message=None. No further action needed for that bug.
WA freeform notifications are live (WHATSAPP_TEMPLATES_APPROVED=false). When templates are approved by Gupshup, set WHATSAPP_TEMPLATES_APPROVED=true to switch to template mode.
WA sends confirmed to dipali's DB phone (919876***) — if test number 9739908844 is expected, verify dipali's phone field in DB.
critical_code_review_comments: mira_service_desk.py line 717: FIXED — was accessing request.initial_message.text without null check; now uses null-safe conditional
auth_routes.py line 331-332: Uses asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task() which is deprecated in Python 3.10+. Should use asyncio.create_task() within an async context or BackgroundTasks
server.py line 20041-20043: Same issue — asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task() deprecated. Both WA and Email tasks fire correctly now but may warn in future Python versions
mira_service_desk.py line 766-771: Same pattern. Works but consider background_tasks (FastAPI) for cleaner fire-and-forget
whatsapp_service.py TEMPLATES_APPROVED is read at module import time — changing the env var at runtime won't update it without server restart

bug_fix.description: attach_or_create_ticket returns 500 when force_new=True and no initial_message provided
bug_fix.root_cause: Code accessed request.initial_message.text unconditionally. When initial_message=None (optional field), this raises AttributeError
bug_fix.fix: Changed to null-safe conditional: uses (request.initial_message.text if request.initial_message else '') with correct precedence for mira_briefing

wa_notification_logs.evidence: [WA-CONCIERGE] ✅ Sent to 919876*** | ID: d4dd5010... (3 WA sends), [EMAIL-CONCIERGE_REQUEST] ✅ Sent to dipali@clubconcierge.in | ID: bfb2507a... (3 email sends)

seed_data_creation: Created/deleted test memory entry test-notif-plumb-001. Created 3 test tickets (celebrate/care/birthday_party) during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 219 tested notification plumbing. All 37 tests pass. Key finding: attach_or_create_ticket 500 bug fixed (initial_message null check). WA service sends freeform messages (WHATSAPP_TEMPLATES_APPROVED=false). Email service sends via Resend. Both confirmed working from backend logs. MiraOSPage.jsx pet flickering fix (loadUserPets useEffect) and proactive alerts URL confirmed correct. Next testing should verify: (1) actual WA delivery to 9739908844, (2) welcome email/WA on new user signup flow end-to-end, (3) payment WA/email via Razorpay test payment.

attach_or_create_ticket_500.root_cause: Line 717 ternary expression `request.initial_message.text` was accessed as the 'else' branch even when initial_message=None. Python evaluates the full expression including both branches of ternary before checking the condition when the condition itself requires the value.
attach_or_create_ticket_500.fix: Changed to null-safe: (f'...' if mira_briefing else ...) if request.initial_message else (mira_briefing or '')
attach_or_create_ticket_500.trigger: API call with force_new=True and no initial_message field in payload
